api: Appcues REST API v2 (https://api.appcues.com/v2, documented at https://api.appcues.com/v2/docs)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against the live API reference (https://api.appcues.com/v2/docs, fetched 2026-07-04; the wave2 api_surface.json's /accounts/{account_id}/users, /events, /step-groups entries did not correspond to any real Appcues endpoint — the real per-user surface is path-scoped to an individual user_id, not a listable collection, corrected below). Every practical GET list resource is now a stream (12 total) and every dialect-expressible mutation is a writes.json action (34 total: 9 experience-type publish/unpublish pairs, segment CRUD + membership management, SDK-key CRUD + 2 toggle pairs, end-user profile update/delete + event tracking, group profile update + user association). Appcues has NO detail-by-id GET this connector needs beyond what its list streams already return (every list response already carries the full record shape) — every {resource}/{id} GET below is excluded as duplicate_of its list stream, not overlooked. Import/export bulk-data endpoints, per-user/per-group event-history reads, and screenshots are excluded as out-of-scope depth cuts (rationale on each entry) — none require elevated scope or are technically inexpressible, they were simply not prioritized for this wave.

excluded.reason: single-flow detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the flows list stream
single-Flows-2.0-experience detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the flows_v2 list stream
single-segment detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the segments list stream

kicks off an async bulk membership-export job (returns a job_id polled via the jobs resource, then a downloadable file); the multi-step async-export shape has no single-request declarative equivalent, matching the same class of gap documented for Amplitude's cohort-download protocol
single-tag detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the tags list stream

per-user detail GET requiring an already-known user_id as input; Appcues publishes no list-all-users endpoint (a CDP-style privacy posture), so there is no id source to drive a list stream or a fan_out — reading a specific user's profile is only reachable once an operator already has that user_id from elsewhere (e.g. their own product's user table), which is out of this connector's read-stream scope

per-user event-history GET requiring an already-known user_id; same no-list-source rationale as the user profile GET above — not implementable as a stream without an id enumeration endpoint this API does not provide
per-group detail GET requiring an already-known group_id; Appcues publishes no list-all-groups endpoint, same no-id-source rationale as the user profile GET
single-checklist detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the checklists list stream
single-banner detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the banners list stream
single-pin detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the pins list stream
single-mobile-experience detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the mobile_experiences list stream
single-launchpad detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the launchpads list stream
single-embed detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the embeds list stream
single-NPS-2.0-survey detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the nps list stream

bulk user-profile import (an arbitrary-size array of profile objects in one request); a bulk-import operation outside the per-record create/update/delete write model, same class as Amplitude's cohort upload
bulk group-profile import; same bulk-array-payload rationale as the profile import above
bulk event import; same bulk-array-payload rationale as the profile import above
kicks off an async bulk event-export job (job_id polled via the jobs resource); same multi-step async-export shape as segment_membership_export

single-job detail GET (adds a progress-events array); the jobs list stream already surfaces every job's id/name/status/started_at/url, and job progress detail is a polling concern outside this connector's read model
returns rendered screenshot images/URLs of flow steps for internal QA tooling, not structured analytics/audience/configuration data; no clear reverse-ETL or analytics destination use case

account-level data-ingestion filter configuration; a narrow operational-config resource not prioritized for this wave (no fixture-verifiable response shape published in the reference beyond 'array of rule objects')
mutation for an out-of-scope resource (ingestion_filtering_rules)
